models.code:
class Customer:
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    name = Column(String, nullable=False)
    credit_limit = Column(DECIMAL, nullable=False)
    balance = Column(DECIMAL)


class Order:
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    customer_id = Column(ForeignKey('customer.id'))
    notes = Column(String)
    date_shipped = Column(DateTime)
    amount_total = Column(DECIMAL)


class Item:
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    order_id = Column(ForeignKey('order.id'))
    product_id = Column(ForeignKey('product.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    unit_price = Column(DECIMAL, nullable=False)
    amount = Column(DECIMAL)


class Product:
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    name = Column(String, nullable=False)
    unit_price = Column(DECIMAL, nullable=False)
models.description: Models for customer, order, items, and product.
models.name: Order Management System

models.code:
CREATE TABLE customer (
    id INTEGER NOT NULL, 
    name VARCHAR NOT NULL, 
    credit_limit NUMERIC NOT NULL, 
    balance NUMERIC, 
    PRIMARY KEY (id)
)
models.name: Customer

models.code:
CREATE TABLE order (
    id INTEGER NOT NULL, 
    customer_id INTEGER, 
    notes VARCHAR, 
    date_shipped DATETIME, 
    amount_total NUMERIC, 
    PRIMARY KEY (id)
)
models.name: Order

models.code:
CREATE TABLE item (
    id INTEGER NOT NULL, 
    order_id INTEGER, 
    product_id INTEGER, 
    quantity INTEGER NOT NULL, 
    unit_price NUMERIC NOT NULL, 
    amount NUMERIC, 
    PRIMARY KEY (id)
)
models.name: Item

models.code:
CREATE TABLE product (
    id INTEGER NOT NULL, 
    name VARCHAR NOT NULL, 
    unit_price NUMERIC NOT NULL, 
    PRIMARY KEY (id)
)
models.name: Product

rules.name: Check Credit: Balance Constraint
rules.description: Customer's balance should be less than the credit limit.
rules.code: Rule.constraint(validate=Customer, as_condition=lambda row: row.balance <= row.credit_limit, error_msg="Customer balance ({row.balance}) exceeds credit limit ({row.credit_limit})")

rules.name: Check Credit: Balance Sum
rules.description: Customer's balance is the sum of Orders where date_shipped is null.
rules.code: Rule.sum(derive=Customer.balance, as_sum_of=Order.amount_total, where=lambda row: row.date_shipped is None)

rules.name: Check Credit: Order Amount Total
rules.description: Order's amount_total is the sum of Item amount.

rules.name: Check Credit: Item Amount Formula
rules.description: Item amount is calculated as quantity * unit_price.

rules.name: Check Credit: Copy Unit Price
rules.description: Copy Item unit_price from Product unit_price.

rules.name: App Integration: Order Shipping Event
rules.description: Send Order to Kafka if date_shipped is not None.
rules.code: Rule.after_flush_row_event(on_class=Order, calling=kafka_producer.send_row_to_kafka, if_condition=lambda row: row.date_shipped is not None, with_args={"topic": "order_shipping"})

    order2 = Order(id=2, customer_id=2, notes="Next Day", date_shipped=None, amount_total=0)
    order3 = Order(id=3, customer_id=3, notes="Gift Wrap", date_shipped=None, amount_total=0)

    item2 = Item(id=2, order_id=2, product_id=2, quantity=1, unit_price=50, amount=0)

    product3 = Product(id=3, name="Monitor", unit_price=150)
